author: jtraglia
content: <@&1417820113981145228> The [release action for v1.7.0-alpha.8](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/actions/runs/25925661968) was just started. If successful, it will be ready in ~6 hours. In this time, if we learn that something is missing, we can restart.

author: jtraglia
content: I have a dumb question. When exactly should a builder reveal their payload? My understanding is that they should reveal as soon as they see that 66%+ of attestations support the block. If at `ATTESTATION_DUE_BPS_GLOAS` they do not see enough attestations for the block, they should decide not to reveal the payload. Is this accurate?

author: potuz
content: The honest behavior is to submit as soon as they see the block, if they want to be fancy so be it, but the spec should say that they need to reveal when they see a block

author: jtraglia
content: But not always. The spec does say this:

author: jtraglia
content: I don't fully understand where the 40% number [in this PR](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4807) comes from:

author: potuz
content: *may* is keyword here, and I think that spec also may be very old, from where are you taking this? "payload withheld mesages" sounds like an artifact of my first spec circa 2024

author: jtraglia
content:
That's from here, currently in master:
https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/gloas/builder.md#honest-payload-withheld-messages

author: jtraglia
content: And hmm. Okay so a builder can safely reveal when they see 40% of attestations in favor of the block? Not 66%.

author: potuz
content: That is definitely an artifact from the early draft, withheld message doesn't make any sense anymore. Probably the main text was changed and the title of the section was maintained
